title: Bicillin L‑A (penicillin G benzathine) in short supply: Notice - Canada.ca
content:
## Bicillin L‑A (penicillin G benzathine) in short supply: Notice
Date published: December 19, 2025
Date updated: June 1, 2026
Bicillin L-A (penicillin G benzathine) is a prescription drug used to treat certain infections. It is the main antibiotic used to treat syphilis that does not involve the central nervous system.
In Canada, Bicillin L-A is marketed by Pfizer Canada ULC. Pfizer is reporting a shortage that is expected to last until February 2027 due to a manufacturing disruption.
We recognize the importance of having Bicillin L-A available for patients. Addressing this shortage is a top priority.
We are working with Pfizer to monitor the re-supply of Bicillin L-A.
Beginning in February 2026, we allowed Septa Pharmaceuticals Inc. to import Portuguese-authorized Lentocilin S 1200 (benzathine benzylpenicillin). The company is permitted to do so until February 26, 2027. Benzathine benzylpenicillin is another name for penicillin G benzathine.
We're communicating with health care providers, provincial and territorial governments, the Public Health Agency of Canada (PHAC) and Indigenous Services Canada to share information about this shortage.
For more information, visit the list of drugs for exceptional importation and sale .
Speak to your health care provider if you have questions or concerns about this shortage.
For more information about the management of syphilis, consult the Syphilis guide: Treatment and follow-up .
Check this webpage regularly as we will update it if new information becomes available.
Learn more about drug shortages in Canada and how Canadians can deal with drug shortages .
